test_suite.details: node test.js — 3,518 passed, 0 failed (Sections 1–9 cover wins.json schema, HTML output consistency, internal links, build pipeline, data cross-references, sections.json schema, prose content integrity, prediction panels, Phase A design refresh).

internal_anchors.details: 12 ds-tab-content tabs (overview, evaluate, model, selftest, wins, pages, killshots, timestamp, predictions, falsify, ai, refs); 12 nav-bar showTab buttons all map to existing tabs; 71 id="winNNN" detail anchors match all 71 wins.json IDs; tab anchors fully covered by test.js Section 3.

tab_structure.details: 12 tabs, 0 orphans, 0 nav buttons pointing to nonexistent tabs, active-on-load = overview. The codebase no longer uses sectionNav() prev/next chain — that helper was retired in favor of the tab-bar nav model.

heading_hierarchy.details: 0 skipped heading levels, no h2 before h1 within any tab.

broken: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/raw-text/03-predictions.txt → 404 (dome author appears to have removed the raw-text mirror path; referenced once in data/sections.json part4 ~ 9-station eclipse table)
timeouts: https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ → 000 (TLS handshake aborted — sandbox-side TLS issue, site resolves and serves correctly to browsers; this is our canonical URL and is verifiably alive)
https://www.chime-frb.ca → 000 (sandbox-side TCP/TLS issue; site is reachable in browsers)
https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/ → 000 (sandbox-side TCP timeout; canonical PO.DAAC root, reachable in browsers; carry-forward from 2026-04-30)
external_links.details: 68/76 returned 200 directly. 1 genuine 404 (dome-side link rot — 03-predictions.txt). 2 NOAA URLs return 403 to curl due to bot-blocking; both are reachable in browsers. 5 return 000 (TLS/TCP-handshake aborts) including our own canonical URL — these are sandbox-side artifacts, not real outages.

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json length=71 (69 dome WIN numbers + 057b/058b paired sub-entries). Verdict tallies in HTML render exactly match wins.json: Refuted by Data 9, Self-Contradicted 14, Misleading 22, Std Model Explains 18, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4 (sum=71). 0 unresolved {{...}} placeholders in docs/index.html. uncounted-failures.json count=10 matches '10 acknowledged failures' in HTML and '0 of 67+' / '69 confirmed' framing in llms.txt. code_analysis breakdown: 70 reviewed / 50 relabels_standard / 69 post_hoc / 3 derives_from_dome.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 71 wins.json IDs have a matching id="winNNN" detail anchor in docs/index.html. All summary rows carry the correct verdict CSS class (v-refuted/v-selfcon/v-misleading/v-std/v-notdemo/v-unfalsifiable). All 71 WINs have tldr_evidence AND tldr_verdict fields populated. Each WIN detail panel contains exactly 2 ds-win-section details blocks (Evidence + Verdict) with non-empty ks-tldr summaries. 8 detail_groups all reference consistent group strings.

win_dome_alignment.details: Fetched john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html (91,686 bytes). 69 unique WIN-NNN numbers (WIN-001 through WIN-069), 100% covered by our review. 8 PROS-NNN aliases (PROS-001..PROS-008) present. The 057b/058b sub-entries are documented in llms.txt and intentionally capture distinct claims that the dome assigned the same WIN number — not collisions on our side.

issues: 21 <h2> headings inside tabs are NOT wrapped in <details>/<summary class='ps-summary|ks-summary'> (e.g., 'How the 71 catalog entries actually land' [overview], '3.1 Verdict Summary Table' through '3.6 Detailed: Misleading and Unfalsifiable' [wins], 'Kill-Shot Verdict Summary' [killshots], 'He timestamps the observations, not the predictions' [timestamp], 'The Dome’s Official Prospective Predictions' [predictions]). Many of these legitimately render leading section headers for tables/scorecards where progressive disclosure would be intrusive — but the wins-tab '3.x Detailed: ...' subsection headers are clear candidates for ps-summary wrapping.
progressive_disclosure.details: WIN-level disclosure (Evidence/Verdict ks-summary/ks-tldr) is fully consistent across all 71 WINs. Predictions: all 14 genuinely_prospective entries in predictions.json have non-empty tldr fields. The ~21 unwrapped section h2s are a partial coverage gap, not a regression vs prior runs.

issues: moderate: 4 hardcoded pastel backgrounds in sections.json part5.html (background:#FFE0B2 ×2, background:#BBDEFB ×2) — color-coded comparison cells, likely intentional but should migrate to utility classes
minor: 14 hardcoded #ddd border colors in sections.json part5.html — all on data-table tr style='border-bottom:1px solid #ddd;'; should use var(--border)
minor: 3 hardcoded grey text colors color:#555 in part4.html, part5.html, part6.html — should use var(--muted) or similar
hardcoded_theme_colors.details: 0 #fff / #ffffff / #f8f9fa / white-named light backgrounds (which would actually break the dark theme). All findings are pastel-color or grey literals where theme variables would be cleaner. No regression.

discoverability.details: docs/llms.txt (5,628 B): contains 'funwithscience.net/dome-model-review' canonical URL, mentions ECM/dome model by name, '71 WIN entries documenting all 69 dome WIN numbers' matches data. docs/sitemap.xml: well-formed XML, 2 <loc> entries (main page + llms.txt). docs/robots.txt: 'Allow: /' + 'Sitemap: https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/sitemap.xml'. data/uncounted-failures.json: 10 entries, all FAIL-NNN, no duplicate IDs, all schema fields present. docs/index.html <head> meta tags: <meta name='description'> present, og:title/og:description present, ClaimReview JSON-LD present.

issues: moderate: 1 ID gap — EXP-052 has no tracker entry and no expansions/EXP-052*.json file. Single isolated gap; no evidence of lost work (would expect at least an orphaned file). Could be a skipped allocation.
expansion_tracker.details: items=280, next_id=282, max_id=281 → next_id > max_id, no collision risk. 0 phantom entries (status=complete with output_file pointing to a missing file). 0 stale pending entries (>48h old). 0 orphaned files in monitor/analyst/expansions/ when matched by id (vs by output_file path — older entries point to suffixed filenames like EXP-004-sec31-32.json while the bare EXP-004.json also exists; matching by id resolves the apparent orphans).

Both within normal <5 unprocessed pipeline-lag threshold; no prefix concentration suggesting a digest filter regression.

All 22 spot-checked file paths exist on disk (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/predictions.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, docs/index.html, build.js, monitor/decisions/{open,closed}-issues.json, monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json, monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-skips.jsonl, monitor/curmudgeon/pending-digest.json, monitor/analyst/processed-proposals.json, monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag, monitor/decisions/morning-briefing.txt, monitor/status.json, monitor/review-state.json, monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json, monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json, monitor/analyst/attention-inbox.json, build-scripts/generate-html.js, build-scripts/digest-reviews.js, monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md). 0 hardcoded numerical counts detected in prose.

Build is byte-reproducible from data/wins.json + data/sections.json + data/predictions.json + data/uncounted-failures.json.

issues_found.description: Fresh curmudgeon c3 review at risk of silent data loss until next workspace-sync run. WIN-026.c3.json (17,746 B) was written 5 minutes before this integrity check and has not yet propagated to git.
issues_found.suggested_fix: No action needed — the next dome-workspace-sync cycle (every 4h, next slot ~12:00Z) will commit it. If still uncommitted at the next integrity run, escalate.

issues_found.description: External data-source link returns 404: https://john09289.github.io/predictions/raw-text/03-predictions.txt — referenced once in sections.json (part4, eclipse 9-station prose) as a 'raw text mirror' of the dome's predictions page. The dome author appears to have removed the /raw-text/ path on his site.
issues_found.location: data/sections.json (part4.html, eclipse 9-station table prose) → rendered into docs/index.html ×1
issues_found.suggested_fix: Either swap the link to a still-resolving dome URL (e.g. https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html) or drop the inline link and keep only the table content; our prose stands on its own without the upstream raw-text mirror.

issues_found.description: expansion-tracker.json has a 1-id gap at EXP-052 — no tracker entry and no expansions/EXP-052*.json file. next_id invariant still holds (282 > 281). Looks like a skipped allocation rather than lost work, but worth noting as the only break in the EXP-001..EXP-281 sequence.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Optional — analyst can git-blame the allocation date (~ around when EXP-051 and EXP-053 were created) to confirm this was a writer-side abandon and not a silently-dropped item.

issues_found.description: 21 <h2> headings inside tabs are not wrapped in <details>/<summary class='ps-summary|ks-summary'>. The wins-tab '3.x Detailed: ...' subsection headers in particular look like they should follow the disclosure pattern; killshots, timestamp, and predictions tabs each have one or two unwrapped section headers.
issues_found.location: docs/index.html (wins, killshots, timestamp, predictions, model, pages tabs)
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider/analyst pass — wrap 3.2/3.3/3.4/3.5/3.6 detailed-verdict headers in details/summary; treat overview scorecard and table-leading headers as legitimate exceptions.

issues_found.description: Hardcoded color literals in sections.json inline styles (4 pastel backgrounds in part5.html for highlight cells, 14 #ddd table borders in part5.html, 3 #555 grey text colors across part4/5/6.html). No actual light-theme breakage (no #fff or background:white); all findings are stylistic-cleanliness only.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Migrate the #ddd borders to var(--border) and the #555 text to a theme variable. Pastel backgrounds (#FFE0B2, #BBDEFB) can stay if they're carrying semantic color meaning, but consider extracting to .data-table-warning / .data-table-info utility classes.

issues_found.description: 32 WINs have new_in_v51=true in wins.json, but the rendered HTML does not put a per-row marker (asterisk / NEW badge / ★) on those summary-table rows. The build does use {{NEW_IN_V51}} as an aggregate count placeholder in prose (correctly resolved to 32), so the data IS reflected — just not at the row level. This is a minor mismatch between the integrity spec's expectation ('asterisk marker appears in the summary table') and the current build output.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a small NEW badge / asterisk to summary-row rendering for new_in_v51 entries, or (b) update the integrity prompt's Section 5 description to drop the per-row asterisk requirement (the aggregate count in prose is sufficient signal). Either resolution is fine; choose one.

issues_found.description: www.noaa.gov returns 403 to curl from this sandbox (bot-blocking by user-agent). The links work fine in browsers and the canonical NOAA data citations elsewhere (ncei.noaa.gov, gml.noaa.gov, kp.gfz.de) all resolve. Informational only.
issues_found.suggested_fix: No action — bot-blocking on a real public site is not a broken link.

issues_found.description: Two harmless workspace-only artifacts persist (carry-forward): monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (0 bytes, accidental tab-completion 2026-04-21) and monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak (historical backup, canonical EXP-202.json present in both workspace and git).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Delete both manually next session. Both are inert — neither participates in any pipeline.

summary: Overall WARN. The published site is structurally sound: byte-reproducible build (0 diff against the source data), test suite green (3,518/3,518), all 12 tabs reachable, all 71 WIN detail panels intact, verdict tallies match wins.json exactly, full coverage of the dome's 69 WIN numbers, llms.txt/sitemap.xml/robots.txt/ClaimReview JSON-LD all valid, 0 parse errors in curmudgeon reviews after mandatory digest regeneration, 0 priority-queue decoupling, 0 workspace-sync direction violations. One MAJOR — fresh workspace-only curmudgeon review (WIN-026.c3.json, 5 min before this run) at risk until next workspace-sync cycle; this is normal pipeline lag and self-heals on the 4h sync schedule. Four MODERATEs: (1) one genuinely-broken external link to a dome-side raw-text mirror that the dome author has removed; (2) one tracker ID gap at EXP-052 (no file evidence of lost work); (3) progressive-disclosure coverage gap on ~21 in-tab section headers (especially 3.2/3.3/3.4/3.5/3.6 wins-tab headers); (4) hardcoded #FFE0B2/#BBDEFB pastel backgrounds for color-coded highlight cells. Minors are all stylistic or sandbox-side artifacts (NOAA bot-blocking 403s; TLS handshake aborts on funwithscience.net/chime-frb/podaac from the sandbox; new_in_v51 per-row marker not rendered though aggregate count is). No critical findings, no build drift, no bypassed adversarial review.
